title: [Roserade] Battle Guide
summary: A [Grass]/[Poison] special attacker and utility [lead] that amplifies weaker moves with [technician] while spreading [toxic-spikes] and [sleep-powder].

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Roserade] is a [Grass]/[Poison] special attacker built a[round] [technician] boosts and hazard support. 125 SpA with 90 Speed and 105 SpD puts it in the midrange of Champions' special threats — not the fastest, not the bulkiest, but flexible enough to [lead], nuke, or pivot depending on the build. The two signature jobs are [leaf-storm] damage dealing and the [toxic-spikes] plus [sleep-powder] [lead].

## Strengths
The [Grass]/[Poison] STAB combo hits [Water], [Grass], [Fairy], and [Ground] for solid neutral cove[rage], and [technician] multiplies weaker tools like [magical-leaf] and [hidden-power] into meaningful damage. [sleep-powder] is the match-up-theft button that converts awkward 50/50s into free turns for the [rest] of the team, and [toxic-spikes] quietly taxes every g[round]ed switch the opponent makes. 105 SpD even lets [Roserade] trade with special pivots in a pinch.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 60/65/105 defensive line means any priority attack — [bullet-punch], [ice-shard], [sucker-punch] — ends [Roserade] immediately. [Ice], [Fire], [Flying], and [Psychic] all hit for 2x, and lo[sing] the Speed race to any scarfed attacker is usually a one-shot. [Steel] switch-ins wall both STABs, so the last move slot has to answer them or [Roserade] has to pivot out.

## Recommended Moveset
[leaf-storm] is the nuclear [Grass] STAB — one hit usually closes a game when prediction is right. [sludge-bomb] is the c[lick]-and-forget [Poison] STAB that punishes Fairies and mirror [Grass]. [sleep-powder] locks down a target on the [lead] turn, and [toxic-spikes] taxes the oppo[sing] switch plan from the very first entry. Hidden-power g[round] can replace [toxic-spikes] on an offensive build to answer [Steel] pivots directly.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the safest [lead] pick; it guarantees [Roserade] survives one shot and gets hazards or sleep down. [choice-scarf] is the offensive alternative, flipping Speed to outrun the mid-90s bracket. [miracle-seed] is the Middle option — it boosts [leaf-storm] without the lock-in cost. [technician] is the must-have ability. Tera [Grass] maxes STAB damage, while Tera [Fire] flips the [Ice] and [Fire] weaknesses when [Roserade] needs to [absorb] a hit.

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair [Roserade] with a [stealth-rock] setter so hazards stack quickly, and bring a [Water], [Rock], or [Steel] partner that can [absorb] the [Fire], [Ice], and [Flying] hits aimed at it. A fast setup sweeper loves the [sleep-powder] turn — any partner that can take a free [Dragon] Dance or Nasty Plot behind sleep snowballs the game. An ally immune to [toxic-spikes] ([Steel] or [Poison]) keeps the hazard game one-sided.

## Play Patterns
On the [lead] turn, [toxic-spikes] or [sleep-powder] usually takes priority over damage. Only [fire] [leaf-storm] once a switch is forced or a kill is confirmed — the SpA drop leaves [Roserade] vulnerable. Save Tera for a telegraphed [Ice] or [Fire] move and scout [Steel] switch-ins with [sludge-bomb] before committing.
